methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Seneca operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at the Series A stage, having raised $60M to date. The comparable universe consists of 8 private companies in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker; no public comps exist in this narrow category.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, given the lack of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peer data. Using total capital raised ($60M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series A step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $120M. The peer-median approach was not applicable; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none met the filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar valuation tier) needed to establish a reliable narrow comparable set of three or more companies.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (median), with a central estimate of $120M. The width of this range reflects estimation uncertainty rather than methodological diversity. Key uncertainties include the true revenue run rate and unit economics relative to the peer set, the durability of Seneca's technology differentiation, and regulatory pathway clarity for its application segment. A fuller peer-median analysis would require either identifying additional same-stage private comparables or reconsidering the use of broader sector multiples at acceptable risk of anchor bias.
